subject: [COMMON][CERTINIT] Generate cert with certInit
commit_message:
[COMMON][CERTINIT] Generate cert with certInit

Some components are http based but want to be usable from outside world.
Instead of dealing with TLS part on the component itself, let's use
certInitializer to generate a secret with the certs which will be usable
by Ingress

Issue-ID: SO-3078
Issue-ID: SO-3237
Issue-ID: CPS-281
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: If166716d159586b1eb94c111e9d3d82a54c2fd6e

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

LGTM but I've a stupid question. Does it work? xD
I'm asking because I'm unsure how k8s is going to behave when we try to configure ingress before this job succeeds and secret gets created?
I'm also thinking if we shouldn't first create this secret using helm and later only update it with right values just to be sure that it doesn't stay as a leftover after we run helm uninstall

@Sylvain
What do you think?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> 
> LGTM but I've a stupid question. Does it work? xD
> I'm asking because I'm unsure how k8s is going to behave when we try to configure ingress before this job succeeds and secret gets created?
> I'm also thinking if we shouldn't first create this secret using helm and later only update it with right values just to be sure that it doesn't stay as a leftover after we run helm uninstall
> 
> @Sylvain
> What do you think?

for Ingress, as long as the secret is not there, Ingress will use autogenerated certificate of ingress controller.
for leftover part, I also thought about it and if we helm undeploy __without__ ns deletion after, there will be leftover.
But K8S doesn't like when you update a resource that doesn't belongs to "you" (so kubectl apply on resources created by helm triggers warning) so I guess we could do via helm hook.
But I'd prefer to merge this and then later see how we can improve...

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > LGTM but I've a stupid question. Does it work? xD
> > I'm asking because I'm unsure how k8s is going to behave when we try to configure ingress before this job succeeds and secret gets created?
> > I'm also thinking if we shouldn't first create this secret using helm and later only update it with right values just to be sure that it doesn't stay as a leftover after we run helm uninstall
> > 
> > @Sylvain
> > What do you think?
> 
> for Ingress, as long as the secret is not there, Ingress will use autogenerated certificate of ingress controller.
> for leftover part, I also thought about it and if we helm undeploy __without__ ns deletion after, there will be leftover.
> But K8S doesn't like when you update a resource that doesn't belongs to "you" (so kubectl apply on resources created by helm triggers warning) so I guess we could do via helm hook.
> But I'd prefer to merge this and then later see how we can improve...

yeah I'm just afraid of breaking gating/daily. Do we delete whole namespace in gating/daily?
